title: System Model: Input–Output Description
summary: This page introduces the input–output description of systems, explaining that a system model is a mathematical expression approximating the dynamical behavior of a system. It focuses on electrical systems, describing how Kirchhoff's voltage and current laws (KVL and KCL) are used alongside element models like Ohm's law to derive input–output relationships. Example 1.16 begins the derivation of the input–output equation for a series RLC circuit.

figures.caption: A series RLC circuit with inductance L = 1 H, resistance R = 3 Ω, and capacitance C = 1/2 F driven by voltage source x(t), with output defined as the capacitor voltage v_C(t).
